Class Warrior

java.lang.Object
core.Piece
pieces.Warrior
All Implemented Interfaces:
Moveable, Stabbable
public class Warrior extends Piece implements Stabbable
Represents a Warrior piece within the Kuroshiro engine. The Warrior serves as the frontline infantry. Its movement closely mirrors a standard chess Pawn: advancing forward one square into empty spaces, with the option to move two squares strictly on its first turn, and capturing enemy pieces one square diagonally forward.

Uniquely, the Warrior acts as a "tank" with a resilient health pool of 2 lives. It can withstand one ranged attack without dying, requiring subsequent damage to be fully defeated. Note: Melee attacks (stabs) and standard movement captures bypass this health pool and capture the Warrior instantly.

    color, hasMoved, position

  • Constructor Details

    • Warrior

      public Warrior(Color color, Position position)
      Initializes a new Warrior piece with full health.
      Parameters:
      color - the Color of the Warrior
      position - the initial starting Position on the board

  • Method Details

    • getLegalMoves

      public List<Position> getLegalMoves(Board board)
      Calculates all legal movement and capture destinations for the Warrior. The forward direction is determined by the piece's color (White moves "up" the board, Black moves "down"). The Warrior can move straight forward to empty squares, and diagonally forward to squares occupied by an enemy.
      Specified by:
      getLegalMoves in interface Moveable
      Specified by:
      getLegalMoves in class Piece
      Parameters:
      board - the current state of the game Board
      Returns:
      a list of valid Position coordinates the Warrior can step to or capture on

    • takeDamage

      public boolean takeDamage()
      Inflicts one point of damage to the Warrior, simulating a ranged attack hit.
      Returns:
      true if the damage was fatal (lives reached 0), false if the Warrior survived

    • getLives

      public int getLives()
      Retrieves the current number of lives the Warrior has remaining.
      Returns:
      the remaining health value

    • isAlive

      public boolean isAlive()
      Evaluates if the Warrior is still alive and active on the board.
      Returns:
      true if lives are greater than 0, false otherwise

    • stab

      public Piece stab(Position target, Board board)
      Executes a melee attack on a designated target position. Because the Warrior implements Stabbable, it captures by physically moving to the target's square. This method handles the immediate removal of the enemy piece.
      Specified by:
      stab in interface Stabbable
      Parameters:
      target - the Position being attacked and landed on
      board - the current state of the game Board
      Returns:
      the captured Piece that was removed from the board, or null if empty

    • getSymbol

      public String getSymbol()
      Returns the unique symbol used to represent the Warrior in text-based rendering. This symbol dynamically shifts based on the Warrior's current health pool.
      Specified by:
      getSymbol in interface Moveable
      Specified by:
      getSymbol in class Piece
      Returns:
      the string "W" if the Warrior is at full health, or "w" if it has taken damage
